behaviors.description: The review input is scoped to the lines that changed, not the full content of each modified file.
behaviors.justification: Unit test of diff parsing logic could pass while the agent ignores the scoped input and still analyzes the full file. The real failure manifests at the integration boundary between diff parsing and analysis. Integration test must verify that downstream analysis respects the scoped input, not just that the parser extracts the right lines.
behaviors.test_description: Test that when analyzing a PR with code changes in multiple files, the review analysis only considers the changed lines within those files, not the full file content. Verify by checking which code regions are included in the generated findings.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration test directly catches if the agent analyzes full files despite receiving scoped input.

behaviors.description: Inline comments are attached to the correct positions in the diff view on the pull request.
behaviors.justification: Line number calculation logic could pass Unit tests. Mock GitHub API in Integration tests could pass. Real failures come from GitHub's specific diff view behavior—how it anchors comments, handles edge cases (deletion-only diffs, context lines, renames). Testing against a mock or local simulation won't reveal real GitHub positioning quirks. Requires real GitHub API interaction.
behaviors.test_description: Test that code review comments posted to a real GitHub pull request appear at the correct line numbers in the diff view. Verify positioning by checking the actual pull request on GitHub or using GitHub's API to confirm comment thread positions match the intended code regions.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: System test with real GitHub API will catch positioning failures that mock API tests would miss.

behaviors.description: The agent identifies at least one real defect in a pull request that contains a known introduced bug.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is about code analysis quality—whether the LLM-based agent can classify and identify real bugs. Unit and Integration tests with mocked LLMs cannot verify true analysis capability. The failure mode is 'the real model fails to detect the defect' which only manifests with real LLM calls. Different LLM runs may produce different classifications of the same code, so non-determinism is inherent.
behaviors.test_description: Test that when given a pull request with a known introduced code defect (e.g., null pointer dereference, missing error handling, logic bug), the agent's LLM-based analysis identifies and reports the defect in its findings. Run multiple times to account for LLM non-determinism.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Agentic test with real LLM is required to verify the agent actually detects real defects; mocked LLM tests cannot validate this.

behaviors.description: Every finding listed in the top-level summary corresponds to an inline comment that was also posted—no findings appear in the summary without a matching inline comment.
behaviors.justification: Unit test of summary generation in isolation (mocking inline comments) could pass even if the real pipeline loses findings. The failure manifests at the integration boundary between posting inline comments and generating the summary. Must test the full posting-then-summarizing flow to catch when findings are dropped or added inconsistently between the two steps.
behaviors.test_description: Test that the complete review workflow posts both inline comments and a summary comment, then verify that every finding listed in the summary comment has a corresponding inline comment posted to the PR. Catch cases where findings appear in the summary but lack inline comments, or vice versa.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration test of the full posting and summarizing pipeline catches mismatches that a Unit test of summary logic alone would miss.

behaviors.description: The full sequence—reading the diff, posting inline comments, posting the summary comment—completes correctly for a real pull request without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: The behavior explicitly requires 'a real pull request', which means real GitHub API interaction (System-level) and real LLM analysis (Agentic-level). Real failures could stem from GitHub API timeouts/failures, LLM API errors, or orchestration breakdowns between the steps. Neither System nor Agentic alone is sufficient; both must work together in coordination. This is a multi-step pipeline requiring real external dependencies and coordination.
behaviors.test_description: Test the end-to-end agent workflow: fetch a real pull request from GitHub, have the agent analyze the diff with real LLM calls, post inline code review comments to the PR, post a summary comment, and verify the entire sequence completes without errors or manual intervention. Verify that all comments appear on the PR and the workflow exits cleanly.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Workflow test integrates real GitHub API and real LLM steps; either mock step would hide real failures in that dependency.
